title: Inclusive Transport for All: Gender, Migration and Intersectionality in Mobility webinar
content: As part of EU Diversity Month 2026, we are contributing to this year’s focus on inclusive labour markets for all with the webinar 'Inclusive Transport for All: Gender, Migration and Intersectionality in Mobility' on 20 May 2026 at 12:00–13:15 (CEST)

title: Commission presents plan to secure Europe's fertiliser supply and food security
content: Today, the European Commission adopted the Fertiliser Action Plan: an initiative to support farmers facing rising fertiliser costs and scarcity, reinforce domestic production and reduce Europe's dependency on imports.

title: EU continues to monitor oil market situation and prepares coordinated response to address the jet fuel supply situation
content: While there are no shortages of fuel in the EU at present, regional supply constraints could arise in the next weeks if the blockage of oil supplies via the Strait of Hormuz does not get resolved – with jet fuel being the primary concern.

title: Strengthening EU farming resilience: study confirms key adaptation pathways
content: A new Commission study confirms climate change as the main driver reshaping EU agriculture and highlights concrete adaptation efforts already underway, offering insights to further strengthen resilience, competitiveness and long-term farm viability.

title: EU helps forge a future for Indian Ocean swordfish
content: The European Union helped secure an important agreement in Malé, Maldives, at the annual meeting of the Indian Ocean Tuna Commission (IOTC), introducing a new measure to better manage Indian Ocean swordfish. The agreement is based on a joint proposal from the EU and Australia.

title: Europe’s youth engage with Commissioners Hoekstra and Micallef on climate resilience
content: Climate change is no longer a distant threat for Europe's youth. It is reshaping their coastlines, their farms, their cities, and their futures.
snippet: Climate change is no longer a distant threat for Europe's youth. It is reshaping their coastlines, their farms, their cities, and their futures.

title: Commission report shows Schengen area continues to be resilient and ready for future challenges
content: The Commission published its fifth State of the Schengen report on 18 May, reviewing developments in the Schengen area over the past year and setting priorities for the year ahead. The Schengen area continues to demonstrate resilience, underpinned by collective efforts at both EU and national level.

title: EMN Cyprus High-Level Presidency Conference on the implementation of the Pact on Migration and Asylum
content: The European Migration Network (EMN) Cyprus High-Level Presidency Conference, ‘Taking stock of the Pact on Migration and Asylum: turning strategy into reality’ will take place at the Filoxenia Conference Centre in Nicosia on 19 and 20 May 2026.

title: Commission announces measures to better protect migrant workers and fight illegal employment
content: The European Commission has recently adopted two new reports on the Employers Sanctions Directive and the Seasonal Workers Directive, assessing efforts to ensure fair working conditions for third-country nationals, prevent migrant exploitation and tackle illegal employment.

title: Commission launches the European Housing Alliance
content: European Commissioner for Energy and Housing Dan Jørgensen is inviting Member States, regions, cities and stakeholders across the Union to join Europe’s first-ever Housing Alliance.
snippet: European Commissioner for Energy and Housing Dan Jørgensen is inviting Member States, regions, cities and stakeholders across the Union to join Europe’s first-ever Housing Alliance.

title: New EMN inform examines digital inclusion and accessibility in legal migration procedures
content: Legal migration procedures are becoming more digital. Learn more about accessibility, inclusion, challenges and support for applicants in the new EMN inform.

title: Sweden sees changes to integration policy and research on migrant youth
content: The Swedish government announces new initiatives to support the integration of migrant children and proposes a new law on the settlement of beneficiaries of international protection. It also proposed limiting access to welfare for newly arrived migrants by introducing new eligibility criteria.

title: The young skipper from Culatra defending his island’s fishing identity
content: Fishing is woven into the identity of the small island of Culatra, but with fewer young fishers entering the profession, it is in danger of being lost. Coming from a long line of fishers, Hugo Padinha was determined not to let that happen.

title: 5 things you should know about nuclear energy
content: As the European Union continues to phase out polluting fossil fuels, more focus is on low-emissions technologies like nuclear to power our economy.
snippet: As the European Union continues to phase out polluting fossil fuels, more focus is on low-emissions technologies like nuclear to power our economy.

title: International Day Against Homophobia, Biphobia and Transphobia: Statement by the High Representative on behalf of the European Union 2026
content: Where inequalities and discrimination persist, democracy cannot thrive. Equality is not optional, it is the bedrock of free and resilient societies.

title: First-ever geographical indication craft name receives EU-wide protection
content: Porcelaine de Limoges from France becomes the first craft and industrial product to receive EU-wide geographical indication protection, benefiting from strong safeguards against misuse, imitation and evocation.

title: Commission seeks feedback on industrial biotechnology and biomanufacturing
content: The European Commission launched a call to gather views on the potential for industrial biotechnology and biomanufacturing in the EU. title: Commissioners Šuica and Brunner discuss migration and border management with EU Mediterranean countries
content: Commissioners Dubravka Šuica and Magnus Brunner attended the MED9 Interior Ministers' Conference, on 18 May. Discussions focused on combating illegal migration and protecting the EU's external border in the Mediterranean region, as well as the implementation of the Pact on Migration and Asylum.
snippet: Commissioners Dubravka Šuica and Magnus Brunner attended the MED9 Interior Ministers' Conference, on 18 May. Discussions focused on combating illegal migration and protecting the EU's external border

title: Commission holds a high-level stakeholder roundtable on the EU ETS review
content: The European Commission brought together industry, civil society and market stakeholders to discuss the future of the EU ETS for 2031–2040, focusing on industrial decarbonisation, competitiveness, carbon leakage protection, investment, energy security and the EU’s 2040 climate target.

title: Commissioner Kos visits Kosovo to reiterate support for its EU perspective
content: On Friday, 15 May, Commissioner for Enlargement Marta Kos will be in Pristina on her first official visit to Kosovo. The visit underscores the EU's unwavering support for Kosovo's European perspective and the need for swift, decisive reforms to advance its path of European integration.
snippet: On Friday, 15 May, Commissioner for Enlargement Marta Kos will be in Pristina on her first official visit to Kosovo. The visit underscores the EU's unwavering support for Kosovo's European perspective

title: New EU law to better protect water enters into force
content: EU rules that update the lists of pollutants in surface and groundwaters have entered into force, aiming to improve water resilience and reduce pollution.
snippet: EU rules that update the lists of pollutants in surface and groundwaters have entered into force, aiming to improve water resilience and reduce pollution.

title: One journey, one ticket, full rights: Commission simplifies Europe-wide travel booking and train travel
content: The Commission has proposed new rules to make seamless travel across Europe a reality.

title: One journey, one ticket, full rights: Simplifying train travel in Europe
content: The Commission has put forward new rules to make Europe-wide train booking and travel easier, whilst also better protecting passengers.
